notes: With-skill responses scored 18/18 expectations passed (100%) across all 3 evals.
Without-skill baseline scored 7/18 expectations passed (38.9%) across all 3 evals.

The skill provides the largest lift on eval 1 (brainstorming with framework): baseline scored only 1/6 (16.7%) vs skill's 6/6 (100%). The 3-angle expansion framework and wow-factor specificity requirements are the key differentiators.

Eval 3 (rubric scoring) shows the smallest gap: baseline scored 4/6 (66.7%). The structured 5-dimension rubric is partially inferrable without the skill, but the skill adds deeper diagnostic insight (e.g., identifying the thumbnail problem as a structural flaw vs. generic advice).
Eval 2 (creativity saves money) shows a meaningful gap: baseline scored 2/6 (33.3%). The key failures were not reframing the premise creatively and not naming the 'creativity saves money' principle.

Grader flagged that some expectations in evals 2 and 3 could be tightened to require greater specificity — particularly around what constitutes 'actionable improvements' and 'more interesting concept'.

Output length correlated with quality: with-skill responses averaged ~3,700 chars vs baseline ~1,680 chars. More depth, not padding — skill responses provided budget breakdowns, comparison tables, and specific wow-factor moments.
